NKJV.1: Whoever loves instruction loves knowledge, But he who hates correction is stupid.
NKJV.2: A good man obtains favor from the LORD, But a man of wicked intentions He will condemn.
NKJV.3: A man is not established by wickedness, But the root of the righteous cannot be moved.
NKJV.4: An excellent wife is the crown of her husband, But she who causes shame is like rottenness in his bones.
NKJV.5: The thoughts of the righteous are right, But the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
NKJV.6: The words of the wicked are, “Lie in wait for blood,” But the mouth of the upright will deliver them.
NKJV.7: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, But the house of the righteous will stand.
NKJV.8: A man will be commended according to his wisdom,  But he who is of a perverse heart will be despised.
NKJV.9: Better is the one who is slighted but has a servant, Than he who honors himself but lacks bread.
NKJV.10: A righteous man regards the life of his animal, But the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
NKJV.11: He who tills his land will be satisfied with bread, But he who follows frivolity is devoid of understanding.
NKJV.12: The wicked covet the catch of evil men, But the root of the righteous yields fruit.
NKJV.13: The wicked is ensnared by the transgression of his lips, But the righteous will come through trouble.
NKJV.14: A man will be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth, And the recompense of a man’s hands will be rendered to him.
NKJV.15: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, But he who heeds counsel is wise.
NKJV.16: A fool’s wrath is known at once, But a prudent man covers shame.
NKJV.17: He who speaks truth declares righteousness, But a false witness, deceit.
NKJV.18: There is one who speaks like the piercings of a sword, But the tongue of the wise promotes health.
NKJV.19: The truthful lip shall be established forever, But a lying tongue is but for a moment.
NKJV.20: Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil, But counselors of peace have joy.
NKJV.21: No grave trouble will overtake the righteous, But the wicked shall be filled with evil.
NKJV.22: Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, But those who deal truthfully are His delight.
NKJV.23: A prudent man conceals knowledge, But the heart of fools proclaims foolishness.
NKJV.24: The hand of the diligent will rule, But the lazy man will be put to forced labor.
NKJV.25: Anxiety in the heart of man causes depression, But a good word makes it glad.
NKJV.26: The righteous should choose his friends carefully, For the way of the wicked leads them astray.
NKJV.27: The lazy man does not roast what he took in hunting, But diligence is man’s precious possession.
NKJV.28: In the way of righteousness is life, And in its pathway there is no death.

ESV.1: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.
ESV.2: A good man obtains favor from the LORD, but a man of evil devices he condemns.
ESV.3: No one is established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous will never be moved.
ESV.4: An excellent wife is the crown of her husband, but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones.
ESV.5: The thoughts of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
ESV.6: The words of the wicked lie in wait for blood, but the mouth of the upright delivers them.
ESV.7: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.
ESV.8: A man is commended according to his good sense, but one of twisted mind is despised.
ESV.9: Better to be lowly and have a servant than to play the great man and lack bread.
ESV.10: Whoever is righteous has regard for the life of his beast, but the mercy of the wicked is cruel.
ESV.11: Whoever works his land will have plenty of bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits lacks sense.
ESV.12: Whoever is wicked covets the spoil of evildoers, but the root of the righteous bears fruit.
ESV.13: An evil man is ensnared by the transgression of his lips,  but the righteous escapes from trouble.
ESV.14: From the fruit of his mouth a man is satisfied with good, and the work of a man's hand comes back to him.
ESV.15: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but a wise man listens to advice.
ESV.16: The vexation of a fool is known at once, but the prudent ignores an insult.
ESV.17: Whoever speaks  the truth gives honest evidence, but a false witness utters deceit.
ESV.18: There is one whose rash words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
ESV.19: Truthful lips endure forever, but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
ESV.20: Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil, but those who plan peace have joy.
ESV.21: No ill befalls the righteous, but the wicked are filled with trouble.
ESV.22: Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, but those who act faithfully are his delight.
ESV.23: A prudent man conceals knowledge, but the heart of fools proclaims folly.
ESV.24: The hand of the diligent will rule, while the slothful will be put to forced labor.
ESV.25: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad.
ESV.26: One who is righteous is a guide to his neighbor,  but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
ESV.27: Whoever is slothful will not roast his game, but the diligent man will get precious wealth.
ESV.28: In the path of righteousness is life, and in its pathway there is no death.

NASB.1: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, But he who hates reproof is stupid.
NASB.2: A good man will obtain favor from the LORD, But He will condemn a man who devises evil.
NASB.3: A man will not be established by wickedness, But the root of the righteous will not be moved.
NASB.4: An excellent wife is the crown of her husband, But she who shames [him] is like rottenness in his bones.
NASB.5: The thoughts of the righteous are just, [But] the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
NASB.6: The words of the wicked lie in wait for blood, But the mouth of the upright will deliver them.
NASB.7: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, But the house of the righteous will stand.
NASB.8: A man will be praised according to his insight, But one of perverse mind will be despised.
NASB.9: Better is he who is lightly esteemed and has a servant Than he who honors himself and lacks bread.
NASB.10: A righteous man has regard for the life of his animal, But [even] the compassion of the wicked is cruel.
NASB.11: He who tills his land will have plenty of bread, But he who pursues worthless [things] lacks sense.
NASB.12: The wicked man desires the booty of evil men, But the root of the righteous yields [fruit].
NASB.13: An evil man is ensnared by the transgression of his lips, But the righteous will escape from trouble.
NASB.14: A man will be satisfied with good by the fruit of his words, And the deeds of a man's hands will return to him.
NASB.15: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, But a wise man is he who listens to counsel.
NASB.16: A fool's anger is known at once, But a prudent man conceals dishonor.
NASB.17: He who speaks truth tells what is right, But a false witness, deceit.
NASB.18: There is one who speaks rashly like the thrusts of a sword, But the tongue of the wise brings healing.
NASB.19: Truthful lips will be established forever, But a lying tongue is only for a moment.
NASB.20: Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil, But counselors of peace have joy.
NASB.21: No harm befalls the righteous, But the wicked are filled with trouble.
NASB.22: Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, But those who deal faithfully are His delight.
NASB.23: A prudent man conceals knowledge, But the heart of fools proclaims folly.
NASB.24: The hand of the diligent will rule, But the slack [hand] will be put to forced labor.
NASB.25: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs it down, But a good word makes it glad.
NASB.26: The righteous is a guide to his neighbor, But the way of the wicked leads them astray.
NASB.27: A lazy man does not roast his prey, But the precious possession of a man [is] diligence.
NASB.28: In the way of righteousness is life, And in [its] pathway there is no death.

NLT.1:
To learn, you must love discipline;
it is stupid to hate correction.
NLT.2:
The LORD approves of those who are good,
but he condemns those who plan wickedness.
NLT.3:
Wickedness never brings stability,
but the godly have deep roots.
NLT.4:
A worthy wife is a crown for her husband,
but a disgraceful woman is like cancer in his bones.
NLT.5:
The plans of the godly are just;
the advice of the wicked is treacherous.
NLT.6:
The words of the wicked are like a murderous ambush,
but the words of the godly save lives.
NLT.7:
The wicked die and disappear,
but the family of the godly stands firm.
NLT.8:
A sensible person wins admiration,
but a warped mind is despised.
NLT.9:
Better to be an ordinary person with a servant
than to be self-important but have no food.
NLT.10:
The godly care for their animals,
but the wicked are always cruel.
NLT.11:
A hard worker has plenty of food,
but a person who chases fantasies has no sense.
NLT.12:
Thieves are jealous of each other’s loot,
but the godly are well rooted and bear their own fruit.
NLT.13:
The wicked are trapped by their own words,
but the godly escape such trouble.
NLT.14:
Wise words bring many benefits,
and hard work brings rewards.
NLT.15:
Fools think their own way is right,
but the wise listen to others.
NLT.16:
A fool is quick-tempered,
but a wise person stays calm when insulted.
NLT.17:
An honest witness tells the truth;
a false witness tells lies.
NLT.18:
Some people make cutting remarks,
but the words of the wise bring healing.
NLT.19:
Truthful words stand the test of time,
but lies are soon exposed.
NLT.20:
Deceit fills hearts that are plotting evil;
joy fills hearts that are planning peace!
NLT.21:
No harm comes to the godly,
but the wicked have their fill of trouble.
NLT.22:
The LORD detests lying lips,
but he delights in those who tell the truth.
NLT.23:
The wise don’t make a show of their knowledge,
but fools broadcast their foolishness.
NLT.24:
Work hard and become a leader;
be lazy and become a slave.
NLT.25:
Worry weighs a person down;
an encouraging word cheers a person up.
NLT.26:
The godly give good advice to their friends;
the wicked lead them astray.
NLT.27:
Lazy people don’t even cook the game they catch,
but the diligent make use of everything they find.
NLT.28:
The way of the godly leads to life;
that path does not lead to death.

WEB.1: Whoever loves correction loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid.
WEB.2: A good man shall obtain favor from Yahweh, but he will condemn a man of wicked devices.
WEB.3: A man shall not be established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.
WEB.4: A worthy woman is the crown of her husband, but a disgraceful wife is as rottenness in his bones.
WEB.5: The thoughts of the righteous are just, but the advice of the wicked is deceitful.
WEB.6: The words of the wicked are about lying in wait for blood, but the speech of the upright rescues them.
WEB.7: The wicked are overthrown, and are no more, but the house of the righteous shall stand.
WEB.8: A man shall be commended according to his wisdom, but he who has a warped mind shall be despised.
WEB.9: Better is he who is lightly esteemed, and has a servant, than he who honors himself, and lacks bread.
WEB.10: A righteous man respects the life of his animal, but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
WEB.11: He who tills his land shall have plenty of bread, but he who chases fantasies is void of understanding.
WEB.12: The wicked desires the plunder of evil men, but the root of the righteous flourishes.
WEB.13: An evil man is trapped by sinfulness of lips, but the righteous shall come out of trouble.
WEB.14: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth. The work of a man’s hands shall be rewarded to him.
WEB.15: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but he who is wise listens to counsel.
WEB.16: A fool shows his annoyance the same day, but one who overlooks an insult is prudent.
WEB.17: He who is truthful testifies honestly, but a false witness lies.
WEB.18: There is one who speaks rashly like the piercing of a sword, but the tongue of the wise heals.
WEB.19: Truth’s lips will be established forever, but a lying tongue is only momentary.
WEB.20: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but joy comes to the promoters of peace.
WEB.21: No mischief shall happen to the righteous, but the wicked shall be filled with evil.
WEB.22: Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh, but those who do the truth are his delight.
WEB.23: A prudent man keeps his knowledge, but the hearts of fools proclaim foolishness.
WEB.24: The hands of the diligent ones shall rule, but laziness ends in slave labor.
WEB.25: Anxiety in a man’s heart weighs it down, but a kind word makes it glad.
WEB.26: A righteous person is cautious in friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
WEB.27: The slothful man doesn’t roast his game, but the possessions of diligent men are prized.
WEB.28: In the way of righteousness is life; in its path there is no death.

CSB17.1:
Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge,
but one who hates correction is stupid. ⓙ
CSB17.2:
One who is good obtains favor from the LORD, ⓚ
but he condemns a person who schemes. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
No one can be made secure by wickedness,
but the root of the righteous is immovable. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
A wife of noble character [1] is her husband’s crown, ⓝ
but a wife who causes shame
is like rottenness in his bones. ⓐ
CSB17.5:
The thoughts of the righteous are just,
but guidance from the wicked is deceitful.
CSB17.6:
The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, ⓑ
but the speech of the upright rescues them. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
The wicked are overthrown and perish, ⓓ
but the house of the righteous will stand. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
A man is praised for his insight,
but a twisted mind is despised.
CSB17.9:
Better to be disregarded, yet have a servant,
than to act important but have no food. ⓕ
CSB17.10:
The righteous cares about his animal’s health, ⓖ
but even the merciful acts of the wicked are cruel.
CSB17.11:
The one who works his land will have plenty of food, ⓗ
but whoever chases fantasies lacks sense. ⓘ
CSB17.12:
The wicked desire what evil people have caught, [2]
but the root of the righteous is productive.
CSB17.13:
By rebellious speech an evil person is trapped, ⓙ
but a righteous person escapes from trouble. ⓚ
CSB17.14:
A person will be satisfied with good
by the fruit of his mouth, ⓛ
and the work of a person’s hands will reward him. ⓜ
CSB17.15:
A fool’s way is right in his own eyes, ⓝ
but whoever listens to counsel is wise.
CSB17.16:
A fool’s displeasure is known at once,
but whoever ignores an insult is sensible. ⓐ
CSB17.17:
Whoever speaks the truth declares what is right,
but a false witness speaks deceit. ⓑ
CSB17.18:
There is one who speaks rashly,
like a piercing sword; ⓒ
but the tongue of the wise brings healing. ⓓ
CSB17.19:
Truthful lips endure forever,
but a lying tongue, only a moment. ⓔ
CSB17.20:
Deceit is in the hearts of those who plot evil,
but those who promote peace have joy. ⓕ
CSB17.21:
No disaster overcomes the righteous, ⓖ
but the wicked are full of misery.
CSB17.22:
Lying lips are detestable to the LORD, ⓗ
but faithful people are his delight. ⓘ
CSB17.23:
A shrewd person conceals knowledge,
but a foolish heart publicizes stupidity. ⓙ
CSB17.24:
The diligent hand will rule,
but laziness will lead to forced labor. ⓚ
CSB17.25:
Anxiety in a person’s heart weighs it down,
but a good word cheers it up. ⓛ
CSB17.26:
A righteous person is careful in dealing with his neighbor, [3]
but the ways of the wicked lead them astray. ⓜ
CSB17.27:
A lazy hunter doesn’t roast his game, ⓝ
but to a diligent person, his wealth is precious. ⓐ
CSB17.28:
There is life in the path of righteousness,
and in its path there is no death. [4] ⓑ

AMP.1:
Whoever loves instruction and discipline loves knowledge,
But he who hates reproof and correction is stupid.
AMP.2:
A good man will obtain favor from the LORD,
But He will condemn a man who devises evil.
AMP.3:
A man will not be established by wickedness,
But the root of the [consistently] righteous will not be moved.
AMP.4:
A virtuous and excellent wife [worthy of honor] is the crown of her husband,
But she who shames him [with her foolishness] is like rottenness in his bones.
AMP.5:
The thoughts and purposes of the [consistently] righteous are just (honest, reliable),
But the counsels and schemes of the wicked are deceitful.
AMP.6:
The [malevolent] words of the wicked lie in wait for [innocent] blood [to slander],
But the mouth of the upright will rescue and protect them.
AMP.7:
The wicked are overthrown [by their evil] and are no more,
But the house of the [consistently] righteous will stand [securely].
AMP.8:
A man will be commended according to his insight and sound judgment,
But the one who is of a perverse mind will be despised.
AMP.9:
Better is he who is lightly esteemed and has a servant,
Than he who [boastfully] honors himself [pretending to be what he is not] and lacks bread.
AMP.10:
A righteous man has kind regard for the life of his animal,
But even the compassion of the wicked is cruel.
AMP.11:
He who tills his land will have plenty of bread,
But he who follows worthless things lacks common sense and good judgment.
AMP.12:
The wicked desire the plunder of evil men,
But the root of the righteous yields richer fruit.
AMP.13:
An evil man is [dangerously] ensnared by the transgression of his lips,
But the righteous will escape from trouble.
AMP.14:
A man will be satisfied with good from the fruit of his words,
And the deeds of a man's hands will return to him [as a harvest].
AMP.15:
The way of the [arrogant] fool [who rejects God's wisdom] is right in his own eyes,
But a wise and prudent man is he who listens to counsel.
AMP.16:
The [arrogant] fool's anger is quickly known [because he lacks self-control and common sense],
But a prudent man ignores an insult.
AMP.17:
He who speaks truth [when he testifies] tells what is right,
But a false witness utters deceit [in court].
AMP.18:
There is one who speaks rashly like the thrusts of a sword,
But the tongue of the wise brings healing.
AMP.19:
Truthful lips will be established forever,
But a lying tongue is [credited] only for a moment.
AMP.20:
Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil,
But counselors of peace have joy.
AMP.21:
No harm befalls the righteous,
But the wicked are filled with trouble.
AMP.22:
Lying lips are extremely disgusting to the LORD,
But those who deal faithfully are His delight.
AMP.23:
A shrewd man is reluctant to display his knowledge [until the proper time],
But the heart of [over-confident] fools proclaims foolishness.
AMP.24:
The hand of the diligent will rule,
But the negligent and lazy will be put to forced labor.
AMP.25:
Anxiety in a man's heart weighs it down,
But a good (encouraging) word makes it glad.
AMP.26:
The righteous man is a guide to his neighbor,
But the way of the wicked leads them astray.
AMP.27:
The lazy man does not catch and roast his prey,
But the precious possession of a [wise] man is diligence [because he recognizes opportunities and seizes them].
AMP.28:
In the way of righteousness is life,
And in its pathway there is no death [but immortality—eternal life].

MSG.1:
If you love learning, you love the discipline that goes with it—
how shortsighted to refuse correction!
MSG.2:
A good person basks in the delight of God,
and he wants nothing to do with devious schemers.
MSG.3:
You can't find firm footing in a swamp,
but life rooted in God stands firm.
MSG.4:
A hearty wife invigorates her husband,
but a frigid woman is cancer in the bones.
MSG.5:
The thinking of principled people makes for justice;
the plots of degenerates corrupt.
MSG.6:
The words of the wicked kill;
the speech of the upright saves.
MSG.7:
Wicked people fall to pieces—there's nothing to them;
the homes of good people hold together.
MSG.8:
A person who talks sense is honored;
airheads are held in contempt.
MSG.9:
Better to be ordinary and work for a living
than act important and starve in the process.
MSG.10:
Good people are good to their animals;
the "good-hearted" bad people kick and abuse them.
MSG.11:
The one who stays on the job has food on the table;
the witless chase whims and fancies.
MSG.12:
What the wicked construct finally falls into ruin,
while the roots of the righteous give life, and more life.
MSG.13:
The gossip of bad people gets them in trouble;
the conversation of good people keeps them out of it.
MSG.14:
Well-spoken words bring satisfaction;
well-done work has its own reward.
MSG.15:
Fools are headstrong and do what they like;
wise people take advice.
MSG.16:
Fools have short fuses and explode all too quickly;
the prudent quietly shrug off insults.
MSG.17:
Truthful witness by a good person clears the air,
but liars lay down a smoke screen of deceit.
MSG.18:
Rash language cuts and maims,
but there is healing in the words of the wise.
MSG.19:
Truth lasts;
lies are here today, gone tomorrow.
MSG.20:
Evil scheming distorts the schemer;
peace-planning brings joy to the planner.
MSG.21:
No evil can overwhelm a good person,
but the wicked have their hands full of it.
MSG.22:
God can't stomach liars;
he loves the company of those who keep their word.
MSG.23:
Prudent people don't flaunt their knowledge;
talkative fools broadcast their silliness.
MSG.24:
The diligent find freedom in their work;
the lazy are oppressed by work.
MSG.25:
Worry weighs us down;
a cheerful word picks us up.
MSG.26:
A good person survives misfortune,
but a wicked life invites disaster.
MSG.27:
A lazy life is an empty life,
but "early to rise" gets the job done.
MSG.28:
Good men and women travel right into life;
sin's detours take you straight to hell.

NIV.1:
Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge,
but he who hates correction is stupid.
NIV.2:
A good man obtains favor from the Lord,
but the Lord condemns a crafty man.
NIV.3:
A man cannot be established through wickedness,
but the righteous cannot be uprooted.
NIV.4:
A wife of noble character is her husband’s crown,
but a disgraceful wife is like decay in his bones.
NIV.5:
The plans of the righteous are just,
but the advice of the wicked is deceitful.
NIV.6:
The words of the wicked lie in wait for blood,
but the speech of the upright rescues them.
NIV.7:
Wicked men are overthrown and are no more,
but the house of the righteous stands firm.
NIV.8:
A man is praised according to his wisdom,
but men with warped minds are despised.
NIV.9:
Better to be a nobody and yet have a servant
than pretend to be somebody and have no food.
NIV.10:
A righteous man cares for the needs of his animal,
but the kindest acts of the wicked are cruel.
NIV.11:
He who works his land will have abundant food,
but he who chases fantasies lacks judgment.
NIV.12:
The wicked desire the plunder of evil men,
but the root of the righteous flourishes.
NIV.13:
An evil man is trapped by his sinful talk,
but a righteous man escapes trouble.
NIV.14:
From the fruit of his lips a man is filled with good things
as surely as the work of his hands rewards him.
NIV.15:
The way of a fool seems right to him,
but a wise man listens to advice.
NIV.16:
A fool shows his annoyance at once,
but a prudent man overlooks an insult.
NIV.17:
A truthful witness gives honest testimony,
but a false witness tells lies.
NIV.18:
Reckless words pierce like a sword,
but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
NIV.19:
Truthful lips endure forever,
but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
NIV.20:
There is deceit in the hearts of those who plot evil,
but joy for those who promote peace.
NIV.21:
No harm befalls the righteous,
but the wicked have their fill of trouble.
NIV.22:
The Lord detests lying lips,
but he delights in men who are truthful.
NIV.23:
A prudent man keeps his knowledge to himself,
but the heart of fools blurts out folly.
NIV.24:
Diligent hands will rule,
but laziness ends in slave labor.
NIV.25:
An anxious heart weighs a man down,
but a kind word cheers him up.
NIV.26:
A righteous man is cautious in friendship,
but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
NIV.27:
The lazy man does not roast his game,
but the diligent man prizes his possessions.
NIV.28:
In the way of righteousness there is life;
along that path is immortality.

NRSVCE.1:
Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge,
but those who hate to be rebuked are stupid.
NRSVCE.2:
The good obtain favor from the LORD,
but those who devise evil he condemns.
NRSVCE.3:
No one finds security by wickedness,
but the root of the righteous will never be moved.
NRSVCE.4:
A good wife is the crown of her husband,
but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones.
NRSVCE.5:
The thoughts of the righteous are just;
the advice of the wicked is treacherous.
NRSVCE.6:
The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush,
but the speech of the upright delivers them.
NRSVCE.7:
The wicked are overthrown and are no more,
but the house of the righteous will stand.
NRSVCE.8:
One is commended for good sense,
but a perverse mind is despised.
NRSVCE.9:
Better to be despised and have a servant,
than to be self-important and lack food.
NRSVCE.10:
The righteous know the needs of their animals,
but the mercy of the wicked is cruel.
NRSVCE.11:
Those who till their land will have plenty of food,
but those who follow worthless pursuits have no sense.
NRSVCE.12:
The wicked covet the proceeds of wickedness,
but the root of the righteous bears fruit.
NRSVCE.13:
The evil are ensnared by the transgression of their lips,
but the righteous escape from trouble.
NRSVCE.14:
From the fruit of the mouth one is filled with good things,
and manual labor has its reward.
NRSVCE.15:
Fools think their own way is right,
but the wise listen to advice.
NRSVCE.16:
Fools show their anger at once,
but the prudent ignore an insult.
NRSVCE.17:
Whoever speaks the truth gives honest evidence,
but a false witness speaks deceitfully.
NRSVCE.18:
Rash words are like sword thrusts,
but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
NRSVCE.19:
Truthful lips endure forever,
but a lying tongue lasts only a moment.
NRSVCE.20:
Deceit is in the mind of those who plan evil,
but those who counsel peace have joy.
NRSVCE.21:
No harm happens to the righteous,
but the wicked are filled with trouble.
NRSVCE.22:
Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD,
but those who act faithfully are his delight.
NRSVCE.23:
One who is clever conceals knowledge,
but the mind of a fool broadcasts folly.
NRSVCE.24:
The hand of the diligent will rule,
while the lazy will be put to forced labor.
NRSVCE.25:
Anxiety weighs down the human heart,
but a good word cheers it up.
NRSVCE.26:
The righteous gives good advice to friends,
but the way of the wicked leads astray.
NRSVCE.27:
The lazy do not roast their game,
but the diligent obtain precious wealth.
NRSVCE.28:
In the path of righteousness there is life,
in walking its path there is no death.

NET.1: The one who loves discipline loves knowledge, but the one who hates reproof is stupid.
NET.2: A good person obtains favor from the LORD, but the LORD condemns a person with wicked schemes.
NET.3: No one can be established through wickedness, but a righteous root cannot be moved.
NET.4: A noble wife is the crown of her husband, but the wife who acts shamefully is like rottenness in his bones.
NET.5: The plans of the righteous are just; the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
NET.6: The words of the wicked lie in wait to shed innocent blood, but the words of the upright will deliver them.
NET.7: The wicked are overthrown and perish, but the righteous household will stand.
NET.8: A person is praised in accordance with his wisdom, but the one who has a twisted mind is despised.
NET.9: Better is a person of humble standing who nevertheless has a servant, than one who pretends to be somebody important yet has no food.
NET.10: A righteous person cares for the life of his animal, but even the most compassionate acts of the wicked are cruel.
NET.11: The one who works his field will have plenty of food, but whoever chases daydreams lacks wisdom.
NET.12: The wicked person desires a stronghold, but the righteous root endures.
NET.13: The evil person is ensnared by the transgression of his speech, but the righteous person escapes out of trouble.
NET.14: A person will be satisfied with good from the fruit of his words, and the work of his hands will be rendered to him.
NET.15: The way of a fool is right in his own opinion, but the one who listens to advice is wise.
NET.16: A fool’s annoyance is known at once, but the prudent overlooks an insult.
NET.17: The faithful witness tells what is right, but a false witness speaks deceit.
NET.18: Speaking recklessly is like the thrusts of a sword, but the words of the wise bring healing.
NET.19: The one who tells the truth will endure forever, but the one who lies will last only for a moment.
NET.20: Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but those who promote peace have joy.
NET.21: The righteous do not encounter any harm, but the wicked are filled with calamity.
NET.22: The LORD abhors a person who lies, but those who deal truthfully are his delight.
NET.23: The shrewd person conceals knowledge, but foolish people publicize folly.
NET.24: The diligent person will rule, but the slothful will become a slave.
NET.25: Anxiety in a person’s heart weighs him down, but an encouraging word brings him joy.
NET.26: The righteous person is cautious in his friendship, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.
NET.27: The lazy person does not roast his prey, but personal possessions are precious to the diligent.
NET.28: In the path of righteousness there is life, but another path leads to death.
